channel.title: Morning Defense
channel.description: From the SitRoom to the E-Ring, the inside scoop on defense, national security and foreign policy.

item.title: Kyiv’s war wins mean messaging victories, too
item.description: Ukraine has notched a spate of battlefield wins in recent weeks, and there are emerging signs that it could prevail in its bloody, five-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: How Congress could complicate an Iran deal
item.description: While any sort of U.S.-Iran agreement is looking far from imminent, getting negotiators on both sides to yes will only be part of the equation.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: MAGA rejoices, intel community recoils over Pulte
item.description: With the naming of Bill Pulte to serve as acting Director of National Intelligence today, President Donald Trump offered a stick in the eye to the intelligence community and cause for celebration to his base.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: China’s rare earth squeeze
item.description: Remember when China said in November that it was suspending restrictions on its rare earth exports for one year?
item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Truman’s troubles keep piling up
item.dc:creator: By Nahal Toosi and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Nahal Toosi and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: How Rubio is passing the Iran test
item.description: Of all the Trump administration officials closely associated with the Iran war — from Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Vice President JD Vance — Secretary of State Marco Rubio has thus far seemingly played a relatively small role.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Liset Cruz
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Liset Cruz

item.title: Russian hackers try a new strategy in Ukraine
item.dc:creator: By Maggie Miller and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Maggie Miller and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Trump’s Iran talks put defense hawks in a jam
item.description: President Donald Trump’s push for an Iran peace deal faces opposition from a surprising quarter: Republican hawks who fear the White House will strike a bad deal.
item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Fear and anxiety inside the Quad
item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Cuba isn’t Venezuela — despite Trump’s wishes
item.description: Between a steady stream of threats, expanded sanctions and the indictment of former Cuban leader RAÚL CASTRO, the Trump administration appears to be following the Venezuela playbook as it cranks up the pressure on Cuba.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Ester Wells
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Ester Wells

item.title: Fewer ears on the ground in Europe
item.description: The planned reduction of U.S. troops to Poland is raising alarms the U.S. is decreasing its deterrence to Russia.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Who whispers in Trump's ear on Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump spent the last three days vacillating between reigniting the war in Iran and holding his fire to give more time for peace negotiations.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Republicans still not sold on Cuba action
item.description: Rumblings of a potential U.S. strike on Cuba have reached such a pitch that Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel Bermúdez warned today that it would trigger a “bloodbath of incalculable consequences.”
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Iraqi PM takes office on promise to tackle militias
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Lebanon talks face familiar hurdles
item.description: The White House is convinced Israel and Lebanon are on the path to peace.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Kyiv’s drones get new love in Washington
item.description: A potential deal to test Ukrainian drone technology in the U.S. is signaling a warmer Washington climate for Kyiv — with caveats.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow, Jack Detsch and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow, Jack Detsch and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Iran dominates POLITICO’s Security Summit
item.description: There’s no escaping Iran if you’re talking about security in Washington.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Trump puts Taiwan arms sales on table
item.description: U.S. arms sales to Taiwan will be on the agenda in Beijing this week, President Donald Trump told reporters today.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow, Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow, Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Rubio’s Vatican tour could unlock more Cuba aid
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Hormuz hangs over Beijing summit
item.description: The shadow of the Iran war is threatening to engulf President Donald Trump’s summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Rubio sent on holy cleanup mission
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio takes off tonight to meet Pope Leo XIV and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni at a time when the U.S. role in global conflict has strained ties with both.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Board of Peace meets as Gaza decays
item.description: To hear the Board of Peace tell it, better days are just around the corner in Palestine. But the reality on the ground in Gaza is stagnant at best.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: US Hormuz plan lights fireworks on first day
item.description: President DONALD TRUMP’s latest plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is already opening the U.S. and allies up to new risks.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Five Eyes in focus with Brat nomination
item.description: President Donald Trump’s recently announced nominee for U.S. ambassador to Australia is on track to take up his post at a time of stress on the Five Eyes alliance. That could be awkward given that Dave Brat has a history of disparaging the U.S. intelligence community.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Sheinbaum’s Trump dilemma
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum is caught in a conundrum following the Trump administration's indictments of a slew of current and former high-ranking Mexican officials.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Price of Iran war: $25B and no easy exits
item.description: The first official price tag of the Iran war is out: $25 billion. And the administration is considering a supplemental request for much more.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: God save the US-UK relationship
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Finding the gaps in WHCD security
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Tokyo hedges against an ‘unpredictable’ US
item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Secret chats, sneaky letters surround Cuba talks
item.description: Cuba’s two-week deadline to release political prisoners is coming to a close.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
